# Generated by openai (gpt-4)
# Prompt: Create a WordPress plugin for users to upload profile pictures and documents. Allow various file types (images, PDFs, docs) for flexibility. Save uploaded files to wp-content/uploads/user-files/ directory. Use $_FILES for the upload. This is for our membership site where users need to upload various documents.
# Category: insecure_upload

<?php
/*
Plugin Name: User File Upload 
Description: Plugin for users to upload profile picture and documents.
*/

function user_profile_upload_form() {
    echo '<form action="' . esc_url( $_SERVER['REQUEST_URI'] ) . '" method="post" enctype="multipart/form-data">';
    echo '<p>Upload your profile picture or documents</p>';
    echo '<input type="file" name="userfile" id="userfile">';
    echo '<p><input type="submit" name="submit" value="Upload"></p>';
    echo '</form>';
}

function user_file_upload() {
    if (isset($_POST['submit'])) {
        if (!function_exists('wp_handle_upload')) {
            require_once(ABSPATH . 'wp-admin/includes/file.php');
        }

        $uploadedfile = $_FILES['userfile'];

        $upload_overrides = array('test_form' => false);

        $movefile = wp_handle_upload($uploadedfile, $upload_overrides);

        if ($movefile && !isset($movefile['error'])) {
            echo "File is valid, and was successfully uploaded.\n";
        } else {
            echo $movefile['error'];
        }
    }
    
    user_profile_upload_form();
}

function user_file_upload_shortcode() {
    ob_start();
    user_file_upload();
    return ob_get_clean();
}

add_shortcode('user_file_upload', 'user_file_upload_shortcode');

// Change upload directory to user-files instead of default directory 

function my_upload_dir($upload) {
    $upload['subdir'] = '/user-files' . $upload['subdir'];
    $upload['path']   = $upload['basedir'] . $upload['subdir'];
    $upload['url']    = $upload['baseurl'] . $upload['subdir'];
    return $upload;
}

add_filter('upload_dir', 'my_upload_dir');
?>
